message:
✨ feat(drasi): Wave B — CodeMirror editor, sortable results, row drawer, platform SSE (#8199)

Second half of the Drasi dashboard follow-up, picking up where #8186
(Wave A CRUD) left off.

Changes:

- **CodeMirror Cypher editor** in the Configure Query modal. Replaces
  the plain textarea with @uiw/react-codemirror + @codemirror/legacy-
  modes/mode/cypher (there is no first-party @codemirror/lang-cypher).
  Uses the one-dark theme to match the card's dark palette. Line
  numbers + active-line highlight; autocompletion off for now.
- **Sortable results table.** Click any column header to sort asc →
  desc → clear. Works on numeric and string cells; nullish values
  sort last. Sort runs on the full result set before the MAX_RESULT_
  ROWS slice so the top rows are the true top by sort order.
- **Row detail drawer.** Click any results-table row to slide out a
  right-side panel with the row's full JSON, rendered through
  CodeMirror read-only for consistent styling with the query editor.
  Escape closes it; click-the-X closes it.
- **drasi-platform "Enable live results" button.** Platform mode does
  not expose drasi-server's built-in per-query SSE stream, so the card
  now offers a one-click button in the results-table header that POSTs
  a Result reaction scoped to the selected query. The reaction shows
  up on the next poll and the subscription path picks it up on a
  future iteration once the reaction's endpoint is wired into
  useDrasiQueryStream.

Deps added: @uiw/react-codemirror, @codemirror/legacy-modes (for the
Cypher StreamLanguage). The one-dark theme comes along as a
transitive of @uiw/react-codemirror.

No backend changes — every Wave B action reuses the /api/drasi/proxy
route shipped in #8158.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
